changes.detail: All five mandatory fields present and valid. work_id=LC-S3-LAYER-C-PAYLOAD-IMPLEMENTATION, branch=sprint3/layer-c-payload-implementation, risk_level=HIGH, execution_model=TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH, change_type=BEHAVIOUR. HIGH is correct: primary file is backend/core/analytics/narrative_report_compiler_v1.py, an Intelligence Core analytics output assembler. Per SOP §10, any work touching Intelligence Core = HIGH by default. TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH required for HIGH risk. ADVISORY: change_type=BEHAVIOUR is acceptable. Technically MIXED because Task 6 creates docs/sprints/LC-S3_...md (non-executable data = CONTENT per SOP §4). However MIXED is governed using BEHAVIOUR controls, which are already required by HIGH risk classification. The distinction does not change governance controls. ADVISORY only — not a stop condition.

build_narrative_payload_v1() confirmed present. Derives top_findings from report_v1.top_findings (line 87), root_cause_v1 from report_v1.root_cause_v1 (line 88). Populates all five section intents with correct intent codes and permitted_source_fields. Wired in orchestrator at lines 2216-2224 — NarrativePayloadV1 is already being built and passed to compile_narrative_report_v1() as named kwarg (orchestrator line 2231). Stop condition CHECK: using NarrativePayloadV1 does NOT require adding top_findings/root_cause_v1 to AnalysisDTO — CLEAR. Path B confirmed.

NarrativePayloadV1 is already imported at line 16 and accepted as optional parameter at line 534. When present, compiler records a payload digest in compiler_meta (lines 548-564) but does NOT yet use payload fields for section content assembly. CONFIRMED: five section assemblers remain unmodified and use raw insight_graph/YAML assets. Full section assembly inventory: (1) retail_summary — _build_retail_summary(idl_bundle, compiler_meta) lines 657: uses IDL records filtered to phenotype_allowed, with frontend_allowed_term check at line 220. Does NOT use top_findings. (2) lead_narrative — assembled from _fired_suboptimal_signal_ids(insight_graph) at line 588, filtered through _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS set (lines 27-31: signal_homocysteine_high, signal_homocysteine_elevation_context, signal_mcv_high) and _SECONDARY_SIGNAL_HINTS set (lines 32-38). YAML entity/pathway/functional docs are the content source. Does NOT use top_findings or root_cause_v1. (3) body_overview — _build_body_overview(insight_graph=insight_graph, ...) lines 635-643: reads primary_driver_system_id, supporting_systems, system_capacity_scores directly from raw insight_graph dict. (4) next_steps_narrative — _collect_next_steps(...) lines 659-665: uses entities_doc and domains_by_id from YAML. (5) clinician_synthesis — _build_clinician_synthesis(...) lines 666-674: uses IDL bundle and YAML docs. Sprint 3 must enrich all five sections to use payload fields.

MATERIAL RISK — Cursor must read this before touching lead narrative assembly. The compiler uses two hardcoded signal ID hint sets: _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS (lines 27-31: signal_homocysteine_high, signal_homocysteine_elevation_context, signal_mcv_high) and _SECONDARY_SIGNAL_HINTS (lines 32-38: signal_ldl_cholesterol_high, signal_hdl_cholesterol_high, signal_non_hdl_high, signal_lipid_transport_dysfunction, signal_triglycerides_high). These sets gate which YAML narrative entities are loaded (include_lead and include_secondary at lines 589-590). If a top_findings[0].signal_id is NOT in _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS, include_lead is False and no lead YAML assets are included regardless of ranking. REQUIRED HANDLING: Sprint 3 must NOT silently replace these hint sets with top_findings-driven routing. The correct approach is ADDITIVE ENRICHMENT — keep the existing YAML asset routing logic intact, then enrich the assembled text with root_cause_v1 / top_findings payload data on top. If the prompt's instruction to 'prefer top_findings[0]' for retail_summary and lead_narrative requires replacing the hint-set routing, Cursor must STOP and report. Replacing hint sets with top_findings routing changes which YAML sections fire and is a BEHAVIOUR change beyond the stated scope of 'making the report feel more composed.' This risk is NOT covered by the prompt's STOP conditions and must be explicitly handled.

_build_retail_summary() uses IDL records filtered to phenotype_allowed (line 220 already checks frontend_allowed_term). It does NOT use top_findings[0]. Sprint 3 requirement says retail_summary MUST use lead finding from top_findings[0]. SAFE IMPLEMENTATION: add a lead-finding preamble from top_findings[0] (signal_id, why_it_matters, signal_state) when NarrativePayloadV1 is present, and append it before or after the IDL-based content. Do NOT replace the IDL-based approach — IDL records carry retail_display_label and severity phrases that top_findings do not carry. The IDL content and top_findings content serve complementary purposes.

NarrativeReportV1 fields (all str with default empty string): narrative_report_version, retail_summary, body_overview, lead_narrative, secondary_narratives, longitudinal_narrative, secondary_systems, next_steps_narrative, clinician_synthesis, meta (Dict). frozen=True, extra=forbid. Sprint 3 scope covers the five named sections (retail_summary, lead_narrative, body_overview, next_steps_narrative, clinician_synthesis). secondary_narratives, longitudinal_narrative, secondary_systems are NOT Sprint 3 scope per prompt. Shape is unchanged — Sprint 3 only enriches field content, not the schema.

ADVISORY: test_report_compiler_v1.py imports compile_report_v1 from core.analytics.report_compiler_v1 — this is the Layer B REPORT compiler (assembles ReportV1), NOT compile_narrative_report_v1 (the Layer C narrative compiler). These are different modules. Sprint 3 must NOT rely on test_report_compiler_v1.py to cover its narrative compiler changes. Sprint 3 must create a new focused test file (e.g. backend/tests/unit/test_narrative_report_compiler_s3.py) covering the five-section payload-driven assembly, claim boundary enforcement, and backwards compatibility when narrative_payload_v1 is absent. The existing test_narrative_payload_wp2.py only covers the payload digest recording (lines 62-76) — it does not test actual section content from the payload.

synthesis.py already carries _layer_b_fields_for_validator_prompt() (WP-2 addition, lines 33-65) which provides layer_b_lead_signal_id and layer_b_hypothesis_ids to the validator prompt. The validator (validator_v2.py) already consumes these in _apply_layer_b_post_checks(). For Sprint 3, NO further synthesis.py changes should be needed — the Layer B field mirroring is complete. Prompt's instruction 'possibly synthesis.py only if strictly required for validator compatibility' should be treated as a last resort. If Cursor finds it necessary to change synthesis.py, it MUST stop and report the exact reason before proceeding. The prior WP-2 audit recorded an ARCHITECTURAL finding for an unsanctioned synthesis.py scope expansion. A second expansion requires explicit justification.

NarrativeClaimBoundaryV1 is sufficient for Task 3. prohibited_claim_patterns carries 10 patterns (lines 82-93). allowed_claim_strength = pattern_and_association_only. allowed_consumer_wording = non_diagnostic_pattern_language. clinician_only_reserved = True. For deterministic prose enforcement, Sprint 3 should: (1) check assembled section text does not contain prohibited_claim_patterns substrings, (2) use only allowed wording tier in consumer sections. Stop condition CHECK: 'claim boundaries cannot be enforced with the current contract' — CLEAR. The contract is sufficient. Implementation is a string-check loop, not a new model.

changes.detail: All 12 stop conditions evaluated: NarrativePayloadV1 exists — CLEAR (narrative_payload_v1.py confirmed). ADR Path B present — CLEAR (docs/architecture/ADR_WP2_layer_b_layer_c_contract_path_b.md confirmed). NarrativePayloadV1 includes section_intents and claim_boundaries — CLEAR (confirmed above). ReportV1 exposes top_findings and root_cause_v1 — CLEAR (report_v1.py:81,85). Using NarrativePayloadV1 does NOT require adding top_findings/root_cause_v1 to AnalysisDTO — CLEAR (Path B confirmed, orchestrator already builds from insight_graph.report_v1). No questionnaire changes required — CLEAR. No Knowledge Bus content changes required — CLEAR. No SSOT changes required — CLEAR. No frontend redesign required — CLEAR. Gemini activation NOT required — CLEAR (deterministic path only). Claim boundaries sufficient — CLEAR (analysis above). Narrative output does NOT require invented medical reasoning — CLEAR. Note: signal hint-set routing ambiguity (see CRITICAL_RISK_SIGNAL_HINT_SETS) is NOT covered by the listed stop conditions but is a material behavioural risk. Cursor must handle as described in that finding.

backend/core/models/results.py — must NOT be modified. backend/ssot/ — must NOT be modified. knowledge_bus/ — must NOT be modified. Frontend report pages — must NOT be modified. Questionnaire files — must NOT be modified. insights[] retirement files — must NOT be modified. mock-mode honesty UI files — must NOT be modified. Automation Bus control-plane scripts (run_work_package.py, golden_gate_local.py, update_cursor_status.py) — must NOT be modified. Sprint 4/5 planning files — must NOT be modified. Stash use prohibited as routine closure convenience (SOP §14). No tooling file leakage (.codex/, AGENTS.md) expected.

Tests that must continue passing: backend/tests/unit/test_narrative_payload_wp2.py (payload digest tests must remain green — compiler change must preserve digest recording), backend/tests/unit/test_llm_validator_v2.py (validator tests unaffected — no validator changes unless strictly required), backend/tests/unit/test_clinician_report_runtime_alignment.py (calls compile_clinician_report_v1 directly — unaffected by narrative compiler changes), backend/tests/unit/test_launch_core_proving_harness.py including test_fingerprint_clinician_primary_concern_head_non_empty (proving harness — unaffected by narrative compiler section changes), backend/tests/regression/test_obs2_questionnaire_exercise_unknown_regression.py (unrelated — must remain green). Note: test_report_compiler_v1.py tests compile_report_v1 (Layer B, different module) — also must remain green but is not a Sprint 3 regression target. New test file must be created for narrative compiler Sprint 3 changes.

Three determinism risks require explicit handling: (1) HINT SET ROUTING — if Cursor replaces _LEAD_SIGNAL_HINTS/_SECONDARY_SIGNAL_HINTS with top_findings-driven routing, which YAML entities load changes. Must be additive enrichment only. (2) CLAIM BOUNDARY CHECK — deterministic prose enforcement must be a non-destructive string check, not a text replacement. If prohibited patterns are found in governed YAML asset text, STOP and report rather than silently editing governed content. (3) SECTION CONTENT SHAPE — NarrativeReportV1 is frozen (extra=forbid). Adding payload-enriched content to sections must not change the field names or schema — only the string values of existing fields.

Replacing these with top_findings-driven routing changes emitted narrative content for signals outside the current hint sets. Cursor must use additive enrichment only.

PROVING ARTEFACT FINGERPRINTS — lead_narrative_head and retail_summary_head in latest_fingerprints.json will likely change as section content is enriched. This is correct and expected. The proving harness should be re-run after Sprint 3 implementation and fingerprints updated — this is not a regression.
